name: LL-37
subtitle: Human Cathelicidin | Antimicrobial Peptide
overview: LL-37 is the only human cathelicidin antimicrobial peptide, a 37-amino acid cationic peptide derived from hCAP18 that exhibits broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity against bacteria, viruses, and fungi while modulating immune responses.
mechanism: Provides direct antimicrobial activity through membrane disruption, promotes wound healing by enhancing keratinocyte migration and angiogenesis, and modulates immune responses without systemic exposure when used topically.

items.name: Chronic Venous Leg Ulcers
items.description: 68% ulcer area reduction demonstrated with 0.5mg/mL concentration in clinical trials.

items.name: Diabetic Foot Ulcers
items.description: Enhanced granulation tissue formation and wound closure.

items.name: Pressure Ulcers
items.description: Accelerated healing demonstrated in animal models.

items.name: Innate Immune Enhancement
items.description: Boosts natural immune defenses against pathogens.

items.name: Antimicrobial Resistance
items.description: Active against MRSA and multi-drug resistant bacteria with >4 log reduction in biofilms.

items.name: Immunomodulation
items.description: Balances immune responses without causing immunosuppression.

items.name: Epithelial Cell Proliferation
items.description: Stimulates skin cell growth and regeneration.

items.name: Angiogenesis Promotion
items.description: Promotes new blood vessel formation for tissue repair.

items.name: Collagen Synthesis
items.description: Supports collagen production for wound healing.

deliveryMethods.overview: Topical application is the most established route, providing direct access to wound sites with minimal systemic exposure.

steps: Apply thin layer directly to wound surface
Cover with sterile dressing if needed
Change per clinical protocol (daily to twice weekly)
Monitor healing progress and adjust frequency

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous injection provides systemic distribution for immune modulation. Limited clinical data compared to topical.

steps: Allow vial to reach room temperature
Add bacteriostatic water slowly to peptide
Calculate dose based on final concentration

keyBenefits: Abdomen, thigh, upper arm injection sites

interactions.notes: Enhanced wound healing through complementary mechanisms.
FICI values 0.25-0.5 against resistant bacteria when combined.
Acts as cofactor for endogenous LL-37 production.
Safe combined use with no negative interactions.
High salt concentrations may reduce antimicrobial activity.
Physical incompatibility, especially with IV formulations.

timeline.effects: Reduced bacterial load, initial wound bed preparation
Increased granulation tissue formation, epithelial migration

stopSigns: Severe injection site reactions with spreading inflammation
Allergic reaction signs (rash, difficulty breathing, swelling)
Signs of wound infection (increased redness, warmth, drainage)
Worsening wound condition or delayed healing
Unusual pain or irritation at application site
monitoring: Injection site reactions and excessive irritation

good: Appropriate gel base compatible with peptide activity
warning: Brief room temperature exposure acceptable if promptly refrigerated
Homemade topical formulations may lack sterility

references.title: Venous Leg Ulcer Trial - Grönberg et al.
references.keyFindings: 68% ulcer area reduction demonstrated at optimal doses in chronic venous leg ulcers.

references.title: Melanoma Phase I Trial
references.keyFindings: Intratumoral LL-37 in melanoma patients demonstrated safety and immune stimulation (NCT02225366).

references.title: MRSA Biofilm Study - Noore et al.
references.keyFindings: >4 log reduction in MRSA biofilms compared to conventional antibiotics.

references.title: Structural Analysis - Wang et al.
references.keyFindings: NMR spectroscopy determining 3D structure of LL-37.

latestResearch.title: LL-37: Master Antimicrobial Peptide Review
latestResearch.summary: Comprehensive review of broad functions from infection to cancer immunity.

quickStats.typicalDose: 0.5-1.6 mg/mL topical gel or 100-150 mcg injectable
quickStats.frequency: Topical: Daily to twice weekly on wounds. Injectable: Once daily subcutaneous
quickStats.cycleDuration: 2-8 weeks depending on wound healing progress
